zotero.abstractNote: Machine learning practitioners invest significant manual and computational resources in finding suitable learning rates for optimization algorithms. We provide a probabilistic motivation, in terms of Gaussian inference, for popular stochastic first-order methods. As an important special case, it recovers the Polyak step with a general metric. The inference allows us to relate the learning rate to a dimensionless quantity that can be automatically adapted during training by a control algorithm. The resulting meta-algorithm is shown to adapt learning rates in a robust manner across a large range of initial values when applied to deep learning benchmark problems.
